title: Paro
desc: Gateway to Bhutan and home to the cliffside Tiger's Nest Monastery (Taktsang) and Rinpung Dzong.

title: Thimphu
desc: Bhutan's capital city — the giant Buddha Dordenma statue, Tashichho Dzong, and a lively weekend market.

title: Punakha
desc: Former capital, home to the breathtaking Punakha Dzong at the confluence of two rivers.

title: Phobjikha Valley (Gangtey)
desc: A glacial valley and winter home of the rare black-necked crane, dotted with farmhouses.

title: Bumthang
desc: Bhutan's most sacred valley, home to some of the country's oldest temples and monasteries.

title: Haa Valley
desc: One of the least visited valleys in Bhutan, only opened to tourism in 2002 — quiet and untouched.

title: Trongsa
desc: Home to the largest dzong in Bhutan and the ancestral seat of the Royal Family.
